name: APT29
description: APT29 is attributed to Russia's Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) and is one of the most sophisticated state-sponsored threat groups in operation. Known for supply chain attacks like SolarWinds and persistent espionage campaigns targeting government agencies, diplomatic entities, and technology companies across NATO countries. APT29 is characterized by its exceptional operational security, use of novel malware, and ability to maintain long-term access.

persistence.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys

CISA AA23-347A: SVR Exploiting JetBrains TeamCity

steps.desc: Compromised SolarWinds Orion build process to inject SUNBURST
SUNBURST loaded as legitimate SolarWinds DLL
Dormant 12-14 days; checks domain, AV, processes before activating
Golden SAML attack to forge authentication tokens for cloud access
Compromised OAuth tokens to access M365, Azure AD
Bulk exfiltration of executive and security team mailboxes
C2 traffic disguised as legitimate SolarWinds API calls
Exfiltration via cloud storage and encrypted channels

diamond_model.adversary: SVR (Russian Foreign Intelligence Service), covert cyber operations directorate
diamond_model.infrastructure: Compromised SolarWinds build server, Cobalt Strike C2, cloud-based staging, anonymized VPS
diamond_model.capability: SUNBURST, TEARDROP, Raindrop, GoldMax, EnvyScout, MagicWeb, WellMess, custom SAML forgery tools
diamond_model.victim: US government (Treasury, Commerce, DOE, DHS), Microsoft, FireEye, ~18,000 SolarWinds customers

cves.desc: VMware Workspace ONE RCE for initial access in parallel campaigns
VMware vCenter RCE exploited for lateral movement
PrintNightmare — used alongside SUNBURST for privilege escalation
JetBrains TeamCity RCE exploited for supply chain access
Citrix ADC RCE used for initial access
Fortinet VPN path traversal for credential theft

detection.title: Golden SAML Token Forgery
detection.content: Monitor Azure AD sign-in logs for tokens with abnormal issuer claims or unusual lifetime durations. Alert on SAML assertions signed by certificates not matching the legitimate AD FS signing certificate. Check for AADInternals or ADFSDump tool artifacts in AD FS server logs.

detection.title: OAuth Application Abuse
detection.content: Monitor Azure AD audit logs for application consent grants with Mail.Read, Mail.ReadWrite, or full_access_as_app permissions. Alert on new service principal credential additions outside change windows. KQL: AuditLogs | where OperationName == 'Consent to application'

detection.title: SolarWinds DLL Anomaly
detection.content: Detect anomalous DLL loading in SolarWinds Orion processes. Monitor for SolarWinds.Orion.Core.BusinessLayer.dll with unexpected file hashes. Network detection of DNS queries to avsvmcloud[.]com subdomains using encoded victim identifiers.

related_actors.name: APT28
related_actors.desc: Both Russian intelligence but different services (SVR vs GRU).

related_actors.name: Turla
related_actors.desc: FSB-attributed group. APT29 operations generally more stealthy.

defense_recommendations.text: Implement software supply chain integrity verification (SLSA framework, SBOMs). APT29 compromised SolarWinds build pipeline.
Deploy Conditional Access in Azure AD/Entra ID requiring compliant devices and IP restrictions for admin access.
Monitor Azure AD sign-in logs for SAML token anomalies. APT29 uses Golden SAML to forge authentication tokens.
Audit all OAuth consent grants. Remove excessive permissions (Mail.Read, full_access_as_app). APT29 abuses OAuth for M365 access.
Enable unified audit logging in M365 with 365-day retention. Monitor MailItemsAccessed from unusual IPs.

ioc_sources.name: CISA AA24-057A IOCs
ioc_sources.desc: SVR cloud access tactics, techniques, IOCs

evidence.text: Microsoft attributes Midnight Blizzard to SVR

legal_actions.title: US sanctions SVR and expels Russian diplomats for SolarWinds campaign
CISA Emergency Directive 21-01 for SolarWinds remediation

threat_assessment.summary: Most sophisticated Russian cyber espionage actor. Demonstrated supply chain compromise capability and advanced cloud tradecraft. Patient, stealthy, and highly resourced.

kill_chain.mapping: Supply chain vendor research, cloud infrastructure enumeration, password spraying at scale
Trojanized SolarWinds Orion update (SUNBURST), EnvyScout HTML smuggling, ISO/IMG lure files
Supply chain compromise, spearphishing with HTML smuggling, trusted relationship abuse
SUNBURST, TEARDROP, GoldMax, MagicWeb (AD FS persistence), WellMess
DNS C2 mimicking SolarWinds API traffic, Cobalt Strike, cloud service C2 via Azure/AWS
Email exfiltration via Graph API, Golden SAML for persistent cloud access, source code theft (Microsoft, SolarWinds)
